title: Aspirin for Alzheimer's Disease Prevention Study
brief_summary: This study evaluates the efficacy and safety of low-dose aspirin in preventing cognitive decline in patients at risk for Alzheimer's disease.
detailed_description: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial to assess whether daily low-dose aspirin (81mg) can reduce the rate of cognitive decline in elderly patients with mild cognitive impairment who are at high risk for developing Alzheimer's disease.

eligibility.criteria: Mild cognitive impairment, APOE4 positive, no contraindications to aspirin

primary_outcome: Change in cognitive assessment scores over 24 months
